audit_items.proof_trace: In clusterReadHandler, after clusterProcessPacket returns 1 (link assumed valid), lines 2195-2196 access link->rcvbuf and write to link. However, clusterProcessGossipSection (called at line 1999 inside clusterProcessPacket) can call freeClusterLink(node->link) when a gossip entry targets link->node — which is the same struct — if that node has PFAIL/FAIL set and the gossip reports a different IP/port. freeClusterLink frees both link->rcvbuf and link itself (zfree), but clusterProcessPacket still returns 1 because no return-value signaling exists from clusterProcessGossipSection, causing clusterReadHandler to read/write freed memory at lines 2195-2196.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A malicious cluster node in PFAIL state (link still active during the timeout/2-to-timeout window, or after reconnect before PONG clears PFAIL) sends a crafted PING message whose gossip section includes the sender's own node ID with a different IP/port; this triggers freeClusterLink on the in-flight link inside clusterProcessGossipSection, after which clusterReadHandler performs a use-after-free and double-free on the released link and its rcvbuf.
